rxn_class_summary: Brønsted acid catalyzed intramolecular [2+2] photocycloaddition of dithiane-substituted alkenes/alkynes under visible-light (405 nm) irradiation via a thionium ion excited state

key_intermediates.name: thionium ion 7 (from Brønsted acid protonation/ionization of dithiane)
ππ* singlet excited state of thionium ion

electron_flow: Brønsted acid catalyst ionizes the dithiane to a thionium ion 7, which absorbs visible light (405 nm) to populate a ππ* singlet state; ring closure onto the tethered alkene followed by intersystem crossing gives a triplet 1,4-biradical 8, whose free rotation around the central C–C bond before spin inversion and C–C bond formation explains loss of alkene stereochemistry to give cyclobutane products.

probe_reagents.name: Z-configured substrate 1j (stereochemical probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Stereo-unspecific [2+2]: cis/trans-6 obtained in 67:33 ratio, indicating loss of alkene geometry consistent with a triplet 1,4-biradical with free C–C rotation

probe_reagents.name: X-ray crystallography of 2g
probe_reagents.observation: Confirmed the relative configuration of the cyclobutane product

probe_reagents.name: UV/Vis absorption of thionium ion (Figure 1)
probe_reagents.observation: Strong absorption indicates a ππ* singlet (S1) is populated upon 405 nm excitation

notes: Alternative singlet [2+2] pathway is implicitly excluded because Z-1j reacts stereo-unspecifically (67:33 d.r.), favoring a triplet 1,4-biradical mechanism over a concerted singlet [2+2]. Dithiane group can subsequently be removed reductively (Raney-Ni → 4) or oxidatively (PhI(OOCCF3)2 → ketone 5/6).
